Test Description: Check cashflow forecast
Check journals posted
Run FS Checker
Obtain understanding of the entity's process for recording sales and assess the design effectiveness of controls relavant to the process.

Obtain sales listing for the period and ensure that the total of sales matches to the trial balance. If separate system is in use for recording sales transactions, ensure that the total sales as per independent system matches to the General Ledger/TB.
Discuss with management on the open projects at year-end and assess how the revenue for the work completed to the year-end has been caculated and recorded.
Perform trend analysis (monthly sales, comparison to prior periods/budgets) to identify any unusual fluctuations.
Review changes from PY if either >PM or 30% and obtain explanation for movement
Assess if management has applied the correct accounting policy (IFRS 15 / UKGAAP) in line with performance obligations.
Using sampling calculator determine the number of samples to tested.
Obtain supporting documents such as invoice, contract and Goods Delivery Note (where applicable) to trace amount recorded in GL to the supporting documents.
Assess how the performance obligation is satisfied as per the terms of the agreement.
For sales in foreign currencies, ensure that the appropriate exchange rate (i.e., on the date of transactions) is applied by management.
Where revenue involves estimates/judgements (e.g., variable consideration, long-term contracts), assess reasonableness and appropriateness.
Consider fraud risk of fictitious sales (e.g., unusual terms, related party sales).
Clearly define the cut-off period relevant for performing the procedure, considering management’s timeline for closing accounts before and after year-end.
Select samples covering pre-period and post year-end and perform testing to ensure that the revenue is recorded in the appropriate period.
Review post year-end returns/credit notes to check if revenue recorded pre-year-end should be reversed.
Review the revenue recognition policy and chart of accounts to confirm that revenue streams are appropriately defined.
Test a sample of revenue transactions to confirm correct classification between product sales, services, related parties, or other categories.
Ensure items not meeting the definition of revenue (e.g., reimbursements, finance income) are not misclassified within revenue.
Compare classification of revenue streams to prior periods for consistency.

Review revenue disclosures for compliance with IFRS 15 and UKGAAP, including:
• Disaggregation of revenue into meaningful categories (e.g., by geography, product, service, timing of transfer).
• Disclosure of contract balances (assets and liabilities).
• Disclosure of significant judgements and estimates in revenue recognition.
Verify that related party revenues are disclosed in accordance with IAS 24.
Assess whether gross vs. net presentation (principal vs. agent) is appropriate (if applicable).
Perform consistency checks between segment reporting, trial balance, management reporting packs, and FS disclosures.
Ensure presentation is aligned with industry norms and regulatory expectations.
Review changes from PY if either >PM or 30% and obtain explanation for movement
Obtain an understanding of the process for recording purchases and CoS, including controls over matching goods received, purchase invoices, and postings to the ledger.
Perform a reconciliation between the purchases/GRN records and the General Ledger/Trial Balance to ensure all costs are captured.
Review whether accruals for un-invoiced goods/services at year-end are identified and recorded (GRNI testing).
Perform cut-off testing around year-end to ensure completeness of accrued costs.
Analytical review: compare gross margin % with prior periods and budget to identify anomalies.
Select samples of recorded cost of sales and trace to supporting documentation (e.g., supplier invoices, GRNs, contracts, inventory issues) to verify validity and accuracy.

For foreign currency purchases, test application of exchange rates to ensure accuracy.
Where allocations or estimates are used (e.g., overhead absorption into inventory), evaluate the appropriateness of methods applied.
Consider fraud risk of fictitious purchases (e.g., unusual terms, related party purchases).
Clearly define the cut-off period relevant for performing the procedure, considering management’s timeline for closing accounts before and after year-end.
Test purchases/CoS recorded immediately before and after year-end, tracing to GRNs and invoices to ensure costs are recorded in the correct period.
Review returns and credit notes issued after year-end to ensure related costs are adjusted appropriately.
Verify that cost of sales is classified correctly and not misstated as operating expenses or other costs.
Review the allocation of direct costs (materials, labour, subcontracting) and indirect costs to ensure consistent and appropriate classification.
Ensure consistency of classification with prior periods and industry norms.
Check that related party costs are identified and correctly classified.
Review disclosures in the FS to ensure compliance with requirements under financial reporting framework.
Confirm that disclosures on cost of sales, inventory, and related balances (e.g., GRNI accruals, purchase commitments) are complete and accurate.
Ensure disclosure of related party purchases is in line with IAS 24.
Perform consistency checks between CoS, revenue, and inventory disclosures to ensure margins reconcile appropriately.
Ensure accounting policies for inventory valuation and expense recognition are clearly presented.
Review changes from PY if either >PM or 30% and obtain explanation for movement
Obtain an understanding of the process for recording admin expenses and assess design effectiveness of related controls (e.g., invoice processing, approval workflows).
Perform reconciliation of expense listings to the GL/TB to Ensure all expenses are captured.
Review accruals for unrecorded expenses and test for completeness.
Perform analytical review by comparing admin expense categories to prior periods and budgets to identify anomalies.

Check accuracy of recorded amounts against supplier invoices and underlying contracts.

Clearly define the cut-off period relevant for performing the procedure, considering management’s timeline for closing accounts before and after year-end.
Test a sample of expenses recorded just before and after year-end to ensure they are recorded in the correct accounting period.
Review accruals and prepayments to ensure costs are recognised in the right period.

Review expense accounts to confirm admin expenses are not misclassified as cost of sales, finance costs, or capitalised as assets (unless appropriate, e.g., development costs under IAS 38).
Test samples to ensure consistent allocation across appropriate categories (e.g., staff costs, professional fees, IT, premises).
Compare with prior periods to assess consistency of classification.
Ensure related party transactions are identified and appropriately classified.
Review disclosures in the FS to ensure compliance with requirements under financial reporting framework.
Confirm that significant categories of admin expenses are separately disclosed where material (e.g., audit fees, directors’ remuneration, R&D costs).
Ensure related party expenses are disclosed in line with IAS 24.

Ensure accounting policies for expense recognition and presentation are clear and appropriately disclosed.

Obtain an understanding of the payroll process and assess the design effectiveness of controls (e.g., approval of new joiners, leavers, and changes to pay rates).

Review payroll accruals and prepayments to ensure proper allocation between periods.
Inspect year-end adjustments for salaries, bonuses, and social security contributions.
Review postings to confirm that salaries, wages, bonuses, and related costs are classified correctly (e.g., admin expenses, cost of sales, distribution costs).
Ensure that employer’s contributions (e.g., pension, social security, payroll taxes) are classified appropriately.

Check for consistency with prior periods.
Review financial statement disclosures to ensure compliance with as required under financial reporting framework.
Confirm that directors’ remuneration and key management compensation are disclosed separately as required under financial reporting framework.
Ensure pension and other post-employment benefit costs are presented and disclosed as required under financial reporting framework.
Cross-check totals per payroll disclosures to GL, payroll records, and supporting schedules.
Ensure accounting policies for employee benefits, payroll, and share-based payments (if applicable) are clearly disclosed.
Review changes from PY if either >PM or 30% and obtain explanation for movement
Obtain an understanding of the processes for identifying and recording other operating income (e.g., grants, rental income, management fees, foreign exchange gains).
Reconcile other operating income per the general ledger/trial balance to supporting schedules maintained by management.
Perform analytical review by comparing income streams with prior periods and budgets to identify unusual variances.
Review minutes of board meetings, contracts, and correspondence to identify potential income streams not recorded.

Select samples of recorded other operating income and trace to supporting documentation (e.g., lease agreements, grant letters, invoices, bank receipts).
Verify that income recorded relates to the entity and represents genuine transactions.
Check accuracy of recorded amounts against agreements, calculations, or confirmations.
Where income involves estimates (e.g., government grants, service allocations), evaluate the appropriateness of management’s methodology.
Test items of other operating income recorded immediately before and after year-end to ensure recognition in the correct period.

Inspect post year-end receipts and assess whether income should have been recognised earlier.
Review postings to confirm that other operating income is not misclassified as revenue, finance income, or exceptional items unless appropriate.

Compare classification with prior periods for consistency.
Ensure related party income is separately identified.
Review financial statement presentation of other operating income for compliance with financial reporting framework..
Confirm that significant categories of other income are separately disclosed where material.

Ensure accounting policies for other income streams are clearly disclosed and consistently applied.

Ensure consistency of classification with prior years.

Review financial statement disclosures to ensure compliance with IFRS and UK GAAP:
• IAS 12.79 requires reconciliation between tax expense and accounting profit × statutory rate.
• IAS 12.80 requires disclosure of unrecognised temporary differences and deferred tax assets.
• FRS 102 Section 29.27–29.32 requires reconciliation of tax expense, deferred tax disclosure, and unrecognised assets.
Ensure current and deferred tax are presented separately in the statement of financial position and profit or loss.
Verify disclosure of key judgements and estimates in line with IAS 1.122 / FRS 102 Section 8.6.

Inspect board minutes, budgets for evidence of unrecorded assets.
Ensure additions are correctly classified between PPE categories (land, buildings, plant, fixtures, etc.).

Review financial statements to ensure PPE is presented separately from other non-current assets.
Ensure accumulated depreciation and impairment are disclosed separately from gross cost.
Verify disclosure of accounting policies, depreciation methods, useful lives, restrictions on title, and pledged assets.
Check disclosure of contractual commitments for acquisition of PPE.
Perform physical verification of PPE (on a sample basis) and reconcile to the FAR.
Inspect asset tags, serial numbers, and location to confirm physical existence.

Test for impairment indicators and if triggered, assess management’s impairment review (recoverable amount = higher of fair value less costs of disposal and value in use).
